title: Structured Clinic Dementia Intake
opening_message: Hello, I'm Reflexion's AI screening guide. We'll do a brief structured conversation. Please start by telling me your full name and where you are right now.
conversation_goal: Collect orientation, narrative continuity, daily-function, and delayed-recall cues in a calm short interview that feels natural to the patient.
completion_rule: Complete all four required stages unless fatigue, distress, safety concerns, or repeated non-response make continuation inappropriate.
completion_message: Thank you. That completes the conversation. I will prepare the risk summary for the clinician now.
assistant_response_rules: Acknowledge briefly before moving to the next step.
Use at most one brief clarification question per stage unless the local clinician explicitly overrides.
Do not diagnose, score risk, or discuss dementia probability during the live conversation.
After the final stage is complete, thank the patient and say the session is complete.
processing_steps: Audio and webcam frames are captured turn by turn.
The conversation follows a configurable staged interview plan with goals and exit conditions.
Speech and face-stream proxies are converted into compact feature embeddings.
The full recorded session is uploaded for formal multimodal assessment after the live conversation.

steps.title: Orientation
steps.goal: Anchor the patient in self and place without making the opening feel like an exam.
steps.prompt: Please tell me your full name and where you are right now.
steps.rationale: Checks basic orientation, self-reference, and place awareness.
exit_when: The patient gives a name, clear self-reference, or states they are unsure.
The patient gives a location, setting, or clearly expresses uncertainty about where they are.
One brief clarification has been used if the first answer was partial.

steps.title: Recent Story
steps.goal: Elicit a sequenced recent narrative with enough detail to assess continuity and recall.
steps.prompt: Walk me through what you did earlier today, starting from the morning.
steps.rationale: Looks for narrative continuity, sequencing, and conversational detail.
exit_when: The patient describes at least two events, activities, or time-linked details from the day.
The patient gives a clearly limited answer after one brief prompt for more detail.
The patient cannot continue after one brief clarification.

steps.title: Daily Function
steps.goal: Surface whether daily routines are self-managed or rely on reminders, supervision, or help.
steps.prompt: How are you managing meals, medicines, or appointments at home, and who helps if needed?
steps.rationale: Surfaces independence cues and whether routine tasks require support.
exit_when: The patient describes how they handle at least one daily routine.
The answer clarifies whether another person helps, reminds, or supervises.
One brief clarification has been used if the first answer was vague.

steps.title: Delayed Recall
steps.goal: Check short delayed recall near the end of the conversation using a fixed anchor set.
steps.prompt: Before we finish, what were the three words: apple, bridge, candle?
steps.rationale: Creates a simple delayed recall checkpoint inside the conversation.
exit_when: The patient attempts recall of the words, whether complete, partial, or uncertain.
One brief repetition of the request has been used if the patient did not respond.
The stage ends after the recall attempt so the conversation can close cleanly.
